title: PLA Navy deploys carrier and destroyer task group near Philippines during Balikatan 2026 exercises
summary: China's Southern Theater Command deployed a surface task group including a Type 055 destroyer, Type 052D destroyer, and Type 054A frigate east of the Luzon Strait, responding to the Balikatan 2026 exercises involving US, Japanese, Philippine and allied forces.
contents:
## PLA Navy Deployment Near Philippines

### Force Composition
China's PLA Southern Theater Command announced deployment of a surface task group east of the Luzon Strait consisting of:
- One Type 055 guided missile destroyer
- One Type 052D guided missile destroyer
- One Type 054A guided missile frigate
- One Type 903A auxiliary oil replenishment vessel

### Strategic Context
The deployment directly responded to the Balikatan 2026 exercises involving 17,000 troops from the Philippines, United States, Japan, Australia, New Zealand, Canada, and France, running April 20 through May 8.

### Carrier Activity
The PLAN aircraft carrier Liaoning transited the Taiwan Strait heading south on April 20. Unverified satellite imagery circulated on PRC social media purporting to show Liaoning operating in the South China Sea alongside three guided missile destroyers and six guided missile frigates.

### Type 076 Sea Trials
Global Times reported the PLAN's new Type 076 landing helicopter dock departed Shanghai to conduct sea trials in the South China Sea, possibly supporting the naval response to Balikatan.

### Scarborough Shoal Patrols
China's Defense Ministry confirmed naval and air combat readiness patrols around Scarborough Shoal, describing them as "effective countermeasures to cope with rights violation and provocative acts."

### Assessment
ISW assessed the deployment as an information operation designed to demonstrate PLAN capability to break out of the first island chain, though peacetime transit does not indicate wartime capabilities.
